texto: We suddenly feel fearful and apprehensive, naked in our perishable flesh, and for just a moment we wish we could go back to being stone—crumbling in death rather than rotting, trapped inside an immobile prison of stone rather than reduced to immaterial souls like those that now rattled within our skulls. The moment passes. There is no point in regretting irreversible decisions—one has to live with them, and we try.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Ekaterina Sedia: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “We suddenly feel fearful and apprehensive, naked in our perishable flesh, and for just a moment w…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que tempo, death, decisions pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de luto e decisions.

texto: Helen devises plans to become a monster herself.

texto: But why?" Vimbai whispered, overwhelmed with the weight of accumulated disbelief. "What is happening to us?""Who knows?" Maya shrugged. "Who cares? Enjoy it while you can, why don't you? There will be tons of boring shit in your life, okay? I promise.

"What is happen…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, fantasy-fiction, weirdness, com foco em weirdness.

frase_original: But why?" Vimbai whispered, overwhelmed with the weight of accumulated disbelief. "What is happening to us?""Who knows?" Maya shrugged. "Who cares? Enjoy it while you can, why don't you? There will be tons of boring shit in your life, okay? I promise.

texto: That was the trouble with the supernatural, Vimbai thought--you didn't know what laws ruled it, and what was a coincidence and what was a sign and what was weird and what wasn't. It was like a whodunit, only the clues refused to be arranged into any sort of hierarchy or a straight narrative, and most of the time it wasn't even clear if they indeed were clues; a jigsaw puzzle where all the pieces were blank.
